title: Introduction to numhelpr
text: Working numbers R often requires writing small formulas . numhelpr solves three common annoyances. Formatting decimals percentages Calculating percent change two values Clamping values within specified range

title: Introduction to numhelpr
text: use built-sales_data dataset demonstrate function.
code: sales_data #>       product price_old price_new rating raw_score #> 1      Widget        10     12.50  0.873       1.8 #> 2      Gadget        25     22.00  0.654      -0.5 #> 3   Doohickey         5      5.75  0.921       3.2 #> 4 Thingamajig       100    115.00  0.102       0.7

title: Introduction to numhelpr
text: Converting decimal rating readable percentage normally requires multiplying 100 pasting % sign. percent() one step. can also control number decimal places:

title: Introduction to numhelpr
text: Calculating percent change old new prices normally requires writing formula every time. percent_change() handles cleanly. Positive values indicate price increase, negative values decrease.

title: Introduction to numhelpr
text: Sometimes raw scores need restricted valid range. clamp() caps values lower upper bound. values 0 set 0, value 2 set 2.

what: Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum — clamp
title: Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum — clamp
text: Restricts numeric values specified range replacing value lower bound lower bound, value upper bound upper bound. Base R built-function common operation.

title: Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum — clamp
Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum — clamp
text: x numeric vector. lower single numeric value specifying minimum allowed value. upper single numeric value specifying maximum allowed value.

title: Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum — clamp
text: numeric vector values clamped lower upper.

title: Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum — clamp
text: Clamping useful many real-world scenarios values must stay within valid range — example, ensuring model predictions stay 0 1, keeping sensor readings within safe operating range, preventing scores exceeding maximum value. Unlike filtering, clamping preserves data points adjusting extreme values boundary rather removing .

title: Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum — clamp

what: Format a Number as a Percentage — percent
title: Format a Number as a Percentage — percent
text: Converts numeric value formatted percentage string multiplying 100 appending percent sign. convenient alternative manually multiplying 100 pasting percent sign. Works numeric input, including decimals greater 1.

title: Format a Number as a Percentage — percent
Format a Number as a Percentage — percent
text: x numeric vector values (e.g., 0.75 75%, 1.5 150%). digits non-negative number specifying number decimal places percentage. Defaults 1.

title: Format a Number as a Percentage — percent
text: character vector formatted percentage strings.

title: Format a Number as a Percentage — percent

title: Calculate Percent Change Between Two Values — percent_change
text: Calculates percentage change old value new value. convenient alternative writing formula manually every time.

Calculate Percent Change Between Two Values — percent_change
text: old numeric vector starting values. contain zeros result division zero. new numeric vector ending values. Must length old.

title: Calculate Percent Change Between Two Values — percent_change
text: numeric vector percent changes.

what: Sales Data for numhelpr Examples — sales_data
title: Sales Data for numhelpr Examples — sales_data
text: small dataset containing product sales information can used practice functions numhelpr package.

title: Sales Data for numhelpr Examples — sales_data
Sales Data for numhelpr Examples — sales_data
text: data frame 4 rows 5 variables: product Name product price_old Original price product, US dollars price_new New price product, US dollars rating Customer satisfaction rating, decimal 0 1 raw_score Raw performance score, arbitrary units

title: Sales Data for numhelpr Examples — sales_data
code: sales_data #>       product price_old price_new rating raw_score #> 1      Widget        10     12.50  0.873       1.8 #> 2      Gadget        25     22.00  0.654      -0.5 #> 3   Doohickey         5      5.75  0.921       3.2 #> 4 Thingamajig       100    115.00  0.102       0.7 percent(sales_data$rating) #> [1] "87.3%" "65.4%" "92.1%" "10.2%" percent_change(sales_data$price_old, sales_data$price_new) #> [1]  25 -12  15  15 clamp(sales_data$raw_score, lower = 0, upper = 2) #> [1] 1.8 0.0 2.0 0.7
